paragraphs: Onboarding Journey + Dynamic Question Flow Specification
DOC-10 | Version 1.0 | Meal Planning RE Technical Docs v1
1. Purpose and Scope
Defines the user-facing onboarding journey that captures a small number of high-signal answers without forcing users to choose from a long persona list.

This document is part of the canonical 28-document technical system. It should be read in the order defined in DOC-00. It is written for an AI coding agent that must implement the agreed recommendation engine exactly, without inventing a competing architecture.
2. Applicable Build Series
3. Core Implementation Invariants
Always build a household profile before generating any plan.
Always map user-facing cohort answers into backend personas and overlays; do not expose all backend personas in onboarding.
Always generate a 7-day class-first plan before selecting dishes.
Always keep primary family meal classes separate from member-specific add-on components.
Never allow kid, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, or recovery add-on classes to replace the main family meal unless the household itself has only that member segment.
Always apply hard constraints before scoring: diet type, allergies, Jain/fasting rules, and Never list.
Always use home state and current city as separate signals; do not collapse them into one location field.
Always use class-to-dish mapping keyed by meal_class_code; never mix dishes from unrelated meal classes.
Use v3 workbook IDs as canonical seed IDs unless a later approved source-data version supersedes them.
4. Onboarding Principles
Ask only questions that change the first-week plan.
Use cards/chips/swipes instead of long forms wherever possible.
Keep main cohort selection to around 4-5 options.
Ask sub-cohort questions dynamically after main cohort.
Capture home state and current city separately.
Prefer class-level preference signals over dish-level aspirational choices.
Allow skip; use DOC-20 cold-start defaults when skipped.
5. Recommended Screen Flow
6. Dynamic Branching

If the user selects family with kids, ask age-band chips for infant/toddler/school child/teen. If the user selects family with elders, ask soft/digestive/diabetic follow-ups. If the user selects special health/life stage, ask pregnancy/postpartum/diabetes/fitness/recovery. If the user selects solo/couple, avoid irrelevant child screens unless they explicitly add a member.
7. Output Contract

onboarding_result = {  "main_cohort_id": "MC_FAMILY",  "sub_cohort_id": "SC_COUPLE_WITH_INFANT",  "home_state": "Madhya Pradesh",  "current_city": "Mumbai",  "diet_mode": "vegetarian",  "member_segments": ["INFANT", "DIABETIC_ELDER"],  "cook_capability": "assisted_cook_needs_instruction",  "class_preference_swipes": ["BF_LIGHT_GRAIN", "LUNCH_DAL_SABZI_ROTI"]}
8. AI Coding Agent Instructions
Read DOC-00 first to understand build sequence and document dependencies.
Use this document when implementing: BUILD-02.
Treat all IDs in source workbook v3 as canonical seed data identifiers.
When logic and data conflict, follow the rule hierarchy: hard constraints > add-on separation > weekly class matrix > state/city overlay > dish scoring > randomization.
Do not create new meal class codes, persona codes, or enum values in application code. Additions must go through DOC-27 Admin CMS and DOC-28 governance.
Write unit tests based on the acceptance criteria in this document and DOC-25.
9. Acceptance Criteria
Onboarding can be completed quickly while still producing a valid household profile.
Every answer maps to a known backend field in DOC-11.
Skipped answers have defined fallback behavior in DOC-20.
10. Change Log
tables: Defines the user-facing onboarding journey that captures a small number of high-signal answers without forcing users to choose from a long persona list.
Claude / AI coding agent, product owner, backend developer, data operations, QA
Generate meal classes first; expand to dishes second; attach member add-ons separately.
Build dynamic onboarding, household/member capture, diet and city inputs, and profile object.
Who are we planning meals for?
Which option best describes your household?
Any babies, kids, elders, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetes, fitness needs?
Where is your family food from?
Who cooks and how much time do you have?
Swipe meal-class examples, not only dish names.
Child age band, tiffin need, spice tolerance.
Child/tiffin questions unless children also present.
Infant age band and feeding type.
Initial canonical technical document generated from agreed v3 workbook and documentation architecture.
